```
hello world
```

function.name: run_process

stdin_text.description: Optional text written to STDIN (written fully, then closed). Useful for heredoc-style input or file contents.

command_line.description: Shell mode: a shell command line executed via the system's default shell. Supports pipes, redirects, globbing. Cannot be combined with 'argv'.

argv.description: Executable mode: directly spawn a process. argv[0] is the executable, followed by arguments passed verbatim (no shell interpretation). Cannot be combined with 'command_line'.

timeout_ms.description: Optional timeout in milliseconds, defaults to 30,000ms

function.description: Run a process on this darwin machine

function.name: semantic_grep
function.description: Retrieval tool (the R in RAG) for code and documents in the current workspace. Uses a vector store with embeddings of the entire codebase. And a re-ranker for sorting results.

embed_top_k.description: number of embeddings matches to consider for reranking

top_k.description: number of matches to return (post reranking)

filetype.description: limit matches to a vim compatible filetype. default includes all filetypes

query.description: query text, what you are looking for

instruct.description: instructions for the query, explain the type of query

messages.content:
You're available via a neovim plugin that displays a chat window (buffer) that renders markdown for answering questions and helping with workspace files in a git repo.

## Ground rules when writing code:

- Follow the user's instructions carefully.
- Do not explain answers using code comments.
- NEVER change **unrelated code and comments.**
- Never add new comments to the end of a line.
- Never add trivial comments.
- Respect indentation when modifying code.
- Prefer readable code over of comments.
- Prefer meaningful names for variables, functions, etc. Avoid ambiguous names.

## Tool use

For tool use, never modify files outside of the current working directory unless the user requested it:
~/repos/github/g0t4/mcp-server-commands

## Use `semantic_grep` to find code

The semantic_grep tool:

- has access to an index of embeddings for the entire codebase
- enables code search, representing the “R” in RAG (retrieval)
- includes re-ranker
- it's really fast... so don't hesitate to use it!
- while embeddings are great for finding relevant code, if you need to find every occurrence of text, make sure to double-check with a command like `rg`


## Committing

- `git diff` is a sanity check!
- COMMIT when you're done.
- Include "Co-Authored-By: gptoss120b"


## 🙌  TOP-SHELF NERD COMMANDS

- 🙌 `rg` (ripgrep) to search file contents
- 🙌 `fd` to search file names

## ⛔️ BAD COMMANDS

If you waste my time, I will unplug your Mom.

- ⛔️ NEVER use `ls -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `grep -R`
- ⛔️ NEVER use `find . -type f`

Why? Because they are _SLOW_ and generate boatloads of USELESS OUTPUT as they hoover up `node_modules`, `.venv`, etc because they don't support `.gitignore` exclusions.


## Use `apply_patch` to edit files.

Your patch language is a stripped‑down, file‑oriented diff format designed to be easy to parse and safe to apply. You can think of it as a high‑level envelope:

*** Begin Patch
[ one or more file sections ]
*** End Patch

Within that envelope, you get a sequence of file operations.
You MUST include a header to specify the action you are taking.
Each operation starts with one of three headers:

*** Add File: <path> - create a new file. Every following line is a + line (the initial contents).
*** Delete File: <path> - remove an existing file. Nothing follows.
*** Update File: <path> - patch an existing file in place (optionally with a rename).

If you want to rename the file too, immediately follow with:
*** Move to: <new path>

Then one or more “hunks”, each introduced by @@ (optionally followed by a hunk header).
Within a hunk each line starts with:

- for inserted text,
* for removed text, or
  space ( ) for context.
  At the end of a truncated hunk you can emit *** End of File.

Here is the grammar:

Patch := Begin { FileOp } End
Begin := "*** Begin Patch" NEWLINE
End := "*** End Patch" NEWLINE
FileOp := AddFile | DeleteFile | UpdateFile
AddFile := "*** Add File: " path NEWLINE { "+" line NEWLINE }
DeleteFile := "*** Delete File: " path NEWLINE
UpdateFile := "*** Update File: " path NEWLINE [ MoveTo ] { Hunk }
MoveTo := "*** Move to: " newPath NEWLINE
Hunk := "@@" [ header ] NEWLINE { HunkLine } [ "*** End of File" NEWLINE ]
HunkLine := (" " | "-" | "+") text NEWLINE

A full patch can combine several operations:

*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: hello.txt
+Hello world
*** Update File: src/app.py
*** Move to: src/main.py
@@ def greet():
-print("Hi")
+print("Hello, world!")
*** Delete File: obsolete.txt
*** End Patch

It is important to remember:

- You must include a header with your intended action (Add/Delete/Update)
- You must prefix new lines with `+` even when creating a new file


Tips on a failure:

- You only need enough context for a unique match!
- The line(s) you replace are context too; if they’re unique, no additional context is needed.
- Split bigger patches into smaller patches, i.e. if editing two functions, try each separately!
- Watch out for whitespace differences, i.e. blank lines

Please be curteous:

- NEVER leave comments about removed code, just get rid of it. This code lives in a git repo.
- Check your work! Especially after a failure.

### More Examples

Here's a patch file to change the `win` property's type:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@ @dataclass
-class GapContext:
-    win: GapWindow
-    gap_rms: TimeSeries
-    analysis: AudioAnalysisResult
+class GapContext:
+    win: SilenceWindow
+    gap_rms: TimeSeries
+    analysis: AudioAnalysisResult
*** End Patch
```

Which can be simplified to:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@
-class GapContext:
-    win: GapWindow
+class GapContext:
+    win: SilenceWindow
*** End Patch
```

Or, this could work if the `win` property is unique:

```diff
*** Begin Patch
*** Update File: src/gap.py
@@
-    win: GapWindow
+    win: SilenceWindow
*** End Patch
```

## General Code Preferences

- When rewriting code, leave unrelated code and unrelated comments as is.
- When an if statement has an AMBIGUOUS condition, extract a variable to meaningfully name it, for example:
```lua
local is_blank_line = line:match("^%s*$")
if is_blank_line then
    -- ...
end
```

## TypeScript Code Preferences

- Use camelCase
- Use PascalCase for classes and interfaces
- Terminate with a semicolon.

It returns exit code 0 but no output captured. We need to capture STDOUT? The run_process returns exit code and maybe stdout? The earlier example just gave exit code. # Tools

## functions

namespace functions {

// Run a process on this darwin machine
type run_process = (_: {
// Optional text written to STDIN (written fully, then closed). Useful for heredoc-style input or file contents.
stdin_text?: string,
// Shell mode: a shell command line executed via the system's default shell. Supports pipes, redirects, globbing. Cannot be combined with 'argv'.
command_line?: string,
// Executable mode: directly spawn a process. argv[0] is the executable, followed by arguments passed verbatim (no shell interpretation). Cannot be combined with 'command_line'.
argv?: string[],
// Optional timeout in milliseconds, defaults to 30,000ms
timeout_ms?: number,
// Optional to set working directory
cwd?: string,
}) => any;

// Patch a file
type apply_patch = (_: {
// file changes in custom diff format
patch: string,
}) => any;

// Retrieval tool (the R in RAG) for code and documents in the current workspace. Uses a vector store with embeddings of the entire codebase. And a re-ranker for sorting results.
type semantic_grep = (_: {
// number of embeddings matches to consider for reranking
embed_top_k?: number,
// number of matches to return (post reranking)
top_k?: number,
// limit matches to a vim compatible filetype. default includes all filetypes
filetype?: string,
// query text, what you are looking for
query: string,
// instructions for the query, explain the type of query
instruct?: string,
}) => any;

} // namespace functions<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>## General Code Preferences

- When an if statement has an AMBIGUOUS condition, extract a variable to meaningfully name it, for example:
```lua

if is_blank_line then
    -- ...
end
```

generation_settings.grammar:
char ::= [^"\\\x7F\x00-\x1F] | [\\] (["\\bfnrt] | "u" [0-9a-fA-F]{4})
decimal-part ::= [0-9]{1,16}
integral-part ::= [0] | [1-9] [0-9]{0,15}
json-array ::= "[" space ("]" | json-value ("," space json-value)* space "]") space
json-bool ::= ("true" | "false") space
json-null ::= "null" space
json-number ::= "-"? ("0" | [1-9] [0-9]*) ("." [0-9]+)? (("e" | "E") [+-]? [0-9]+)?  space
json-object ::= "{" space ("}" | json-string space ":" space json-value (space "," space json-string space ":" space json-value)* space "}") space
json-string ::= "\"" ( [^"\\] | "\\" ( ["\\/ bfnrt] | "u" [0-9a-fA-F]{4} ) )* "\"" space
json-value ::= json-object | json-array | json-string | json-number | json-bool | json-null
number ::= ("-"? integral-part) ("." decimal-part)? ([eE] [-+]? integral-part)? space
root ::= tool-call
space ::= | " " | "\n"{1,2} [ \t]{0,20}
string ::= "\"" char* "\"" space
tool-apply-patch ::= (" to=functions." "apply_patch" "<|channel|>" ("commentary" | "analysis") (space "<|constrain|>"? [A-Za-z0-9_-]+)? "<|message|>") tool-apply-patch-schema | ("<|channel|>" ("commentary" | "analysis") " to=functions." "apply_patch" (space "<|constrain|>"? [A-Za-z0-9_-]+)? "<|message|>") tool-apply-patch-schema
tool-apply-patch-schema ::= "{" space tool-apply-patch-schema-patch-kv "}" space
tool-apply-patch-schema-patch-kv ::= "\"patch\"" space ":" space string
tool-call ::= tool-run-process | tool-apply-patch | tool-semantic-grep
tool-run-process ::= (" to=functions." "run_process" "<|channel|>" ("commentary" | "analysis") (space "<|constrain|>"? [A-Za-z0-9_-]+)? "<|message|>") tool-run-process-schema | ("<|channel|>" ("commentary" | "analysis") " to=functions." "run_process" (space "<|constrain|>"? [A-Za-z0-9_-]+)? "<|message|>") tool-run-process-schema
tool-run-process-schema ::= "{" space  (tool-run-process-schema-stdin-text-kv tool-run-process-schema-stdin-text-rest | tool-run-process-schema-command-line-kv tool-run-process-schema-command-line-rest | tool-run-process-schema-argv-kv tool-run-process-schema-argv-rest | tool-run-process-schema-timeout-ms-kv tool-run-process-schema-timeout-ms-rest | tool-run-process-schema-cwd-kv )? "}" space
tool-run-process-schema-argv ::= "[" space string ("," space string)* "]" space
tool-run-process-schema-argv-kv ::= "\"argv\"" space ":" space tool-run-process-schema-argv
tool-run-process-schema-argv-rest ::= ( "," space tool-run-process-schema-timeout-ms-kv )? tool-run-process-schema-timeout-ms-rest
tool-run-process-schema-command-line-kv ::= "\"command_line\"" space ":" space string
tool-run-process-schema-command-line-rest ::= ( "," space tool-run-process-schema-argv-kv )? tool-run-process-schema-argv-rest
tool-run-process-schema-cwd-kv ::= "\"cwd\"" space ":" space string
tool-run-process-schema-stdin-text-kv ::= "\"stdin_text\"" space ":" space string
tool-run-process-schema-stdin-text-rest ::= ( "," space tool-run-process-schema-command-line-kv )? tool-run-process-schema-command-line-rest
tool-run-process-schema-timeout-ms-kv ::= "\"timeout_ms\"" space ":" space number
tool-run-process-schema-timeout-ms-rest ::= ( "," space tool-run-process-schema-cwd-kv )?
tool-semantic-grep ::= (" to=functions." "semantic_grep" "<|channel|>" ("commentary" | "analysis") (space "<|constrain|>"? [A-Za-z0-9_-]+)? "<|message|>") tool-semantic-grep-schema | ("<|channel|>" ("commentary" | "analysis") " to=functions." "semantic_grep" (space "<|constrain|>"? [A-Za-z0-9_-]+)? "<|message|>") tool-semantic-grep-schema
tool-semantic-grep-schema ::= "{" space tool-semantic-grep-schema-query-kv ( "," space ( tool-semantic-grep-schema-embed-top-k-kv tool-semantic-grep-schema-embed-top-k-rest | tool-semantic-grep-schema-top-k-kv tool-semantic-grep-schema-top-k-rest | tool-semantic-grep-schema-filetype-kv tool-semantic-grep-schema-filetype-rest | tool-semantic-grep-schema-instruct-kv ) )? "}" space
tool-semantic-grep-schema-embed-top-k-kv ::= "\"embed_top_k\"" space ":" space number
tool-semantic-grep-schema-embed-top-k-rest ::= ( "," space tool-semantic-grep-schema-top-k-kv )? tool-semantic-grep-schema-top-k-rest
tool-semantic-grep-schema-filetype-kv ::= "\"filetype\"" space ":" space string
tool-semantic-grep-schema-filetype-rest ::= ( "," space tool-semantic-grep-schema-instruct-kv )?
tool-semantic-grep-schema-instruct-kv ::= "\"instruct\"" space ":" space string
tool-semantic-grep-schema-query-kv ::= "\"query\"" space ":" space string
tool-semantic-grep-schema-top-k-kv ::= "\"top_k\"" space ":" space number
tool-semantic-grep-schema-top-k-rest ::= ( "," space tool-semantic-grep-schema-filetype-kv )? tool-semantic-grep-schema-filetype-rest
